primary_diagnosis.name: Acute fissuring of chronic plaque psoriasis (mild localised), with reactive erythema

pathophysiology_summary: Chronic plaque psoriasis produces thick, hyperkeratotic, silvery-scaled plaques on extensor surfaces (elbows/knees) that lose elasticity; over an extensor joint, and worsened by dry cold weather and lapsed topical therapy, the plaque fissures deeply on bending, causing sharp pain and minor bleeding. Vigorous rubbing produces a rim of reactive (blanching) erythema that mimics cellulitis. The infection mimics to exclude are cellulitis (spreading non-blanching erythema, warmth, induration, lymphangitis, fever), septic olecranon bursitis (fluctuant tender swelling over the olecranon, painful joint movement) and impetiginised psoriasis (honey-coloured purulent crusts) — all absent here. Management is thick emollient, occlusive dressings, restarting topical psoriasis therapy, and a cellulitis/bursitis safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Thick silvery-scaled plaques fissuring on the elbows after lapsed treatment in dry weather.
Screen for spreading non-blanching erythema, warmth, induration, lymphangitis and fever; absent.
Screen for a fluctuant tender olecranon swelling and painful joint movement; absent.
No honey-coloured purulent crusts or weeping exudate.

Blanching localised rim, afebrile, no pus/induration/lymphangitis (DI-001), no olecranon fluctuance and painless joint movement (DI-002), fissuring of known plaques after lapsed therapy (DI-003) — mechanical fissuring, not infection.
Blanching localised erythema, afebrile, no spreading/warmth/pus — cellulitis not supported (safety-net retained).
No fluctuant olecranon swelling and painless joint movement.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: thick emollient (petroleum jelly) + occlusive dressings, restart topical psoriasis therapy, avoid picking scales, humidify, routine GP follow-up, and a cellulitis/bursitis safety-net. Antibiotics/ED for an uninfected fissure are over-treatment.

red_flags.description: Cellulitis — spreading non-blanching erythema, warmth, induration, lymphangitic streaks, or fever
Septic olecranon bursitis/joint — fluctuant tender olecranon swelling, deep joint pain on movement, or fever

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Treating reactive blanching erythema around fissures as cellulitis and prescribing antibiotics/ED without checking for spreading warmth/pus/fever.
Failing to safety-net the open fissures for secondary infection.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Emollient + dressings + restart topical psoriasis therapy with a cellulitis/bursitis safety-net; same-day review if infection signs develop.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Reflex antibiotics/ED for reactive erythema wastes resources; missing a genuine secondary cellulitis would be dangerous (hence the retained safety-net).
